preFix.title: Building under-stair storage
preFix.subtitle: Frame and line the void beneath a stair flight with a simple cupboard
preFix.excerpt: How to convert the dead space under a stair into a useful storage cupboard: framing the opening, fitting a door frame, lining the ceiling with plasterboard, and finishing with skirting and a painted surface.
preFix.sourceNotes: Newey and Drage, Practical Carpentry and Joinery. Collins Complete DIY Manual.

content.text: The void under a straight-flight staircase is one of the most underused spaces in a house. Framing it as a cupboard takes a long weekend: a stud frame defines the opening, a door liner carries the door, plasterboard lines the sloping ceiling and the back wall, and a simple painted finish completes the job. The end result is a useful tall cupboard at the open end and a lower fixed storage area at the back.

Mark the position of the face frame on the floor and on the underside of the stair strings. The frame face should be flush with the edge of the bottom tread riser. Build the frame from 75 x 50 mm CLS: a sole plate screwed to the floor,

at the door opening, and a head plate at the correct height for the door. The head plate follows the line of the underside of the stair, not horizontal, so the door frame must be cut to the appropriate angle at the top if the opening runs into the sloped section.

Fit 12.5 mm plasterboard to the sloped ceiling and back wall of the cupboard space. The sloped ceiling follows the underside of the stair joists; fix through the plasterboard into the joists with plasterboard screws at 150 mm centres. The back wall boards fix to the wall with plasterboard adhesive and additional screws. Tape all joints with scrim tape and apply jointing compound.
Step 3, fit the door liner and door

Fit a door liner in the opening (see the separate tutorial on fitting a door frame liner). Hang the door: a standard pre-hung door of 686 x 1981 mm or 762 x 1981 mm suits most under-stair openings where the stair has a standard 2.1 m ground-floor ceiling. Fit a magnetic catch or a knob and latch.

Fit skirting board and architrave. Skim or tape-and-finish the plasterboard. Apply a mist coat followed by two coats of emulsion inside the cupboard. Light-coloured paint inside the cupboard makes the space more usable. Fit a battery-powered or wired LED strip light if the cupboard is deep.

attrs.fix: Cut the trimmer studs plumb (using a spirit level) rather than following the string angle. The head plate spans at an angle but the trimmers must be vertical for the door to hang.
attrs.cause: The underside of the stair is not vertical at the frame position; the trimmer studs follow the slope.
attrs.symptom: Door frame not square due to the sloped stair string
